norwegian: Jeg har allerede ___ frokost.
english: I have already eaten breakfast.

norwegian: Hun har nettopp ___ hjem fra jobb.
english: She has just come home from work.

norwegian: Vi har aldri ___ til Oslo med tog.
english: We have never travelled to Oslo by train.

norwegian: De har ___ mange ganger til det restauranten.
english: They have been to that restaurant many times.

norwegian: Jeg har allerede sett den filmen.
english: I have already seen that film.

norwegian: Han har nettopp tatt bussen.
english: He has just taken the bus.

norwegian: Vi har aldri bodd i en leilighet før.
english: We have never lived in an apartment before.

norwegian: De har mange ganger gått tur i fjellet.
english: They have gone hiking in the mountains many times.

norwegian: Vi ___ møte deg på stasjonen klokken tre.
english: We will meet you at the station at three o'clock.

norwegian: Jeg ___ kjøpe ny jakke i morgen.
english: I am going to buy a new jacket tomorrow.

norwegian: Det ___ bli mye snø i vinter.
english: There is going to be a lot of snow this winter.

norwegian: Han ___ ikke komme på festen i kveld.
english: He will not come to the party tonight.

norwegian: De skal reise til Bergen neste uke.
english: They are going to travel to Bergen next week.

norwegian: Jeg vil gjerne ha en kopp kaffe.
english: I would like to have a cup of coffee.

norwegian: Det kommer til å bli varmt i helgen.
english: It is going to be warm this weekend.

norwegian: Hun skal begynne på universitetet til høsten.
english: She is going to start university in the autumn.

norwegian: Vi kommer til å trenge mer tid for å fullføre prosjektet.
english: We are going to need more time to finish the project.

norwegian: Huset ble ___ forrige måned.
english: The house was sold last month.

norwegian: Boken ___ av mange studenter hvert år.
english: The book is read by many students every year.

norwegian: Filmen ___ av tusenvis av folk hvert år.
english: The film is seen by thousands of people every year.

norwegian: Maten ___ ferdig for en time siden.
english: The food was prepared an hour ago.

norwegian: Vinduet ble knust av en ball.
english: The window was broken by a ball.

norwegian: Norsk snakkes av mange mennesker i verden.
english: Norwegian is spoken by many people in the world.

norwegian: Brevet ble sendt i går morges.
english: The letter was sent yesterday morning.

norwegian: Parken brukes av folk i alle aldre.
english: The park is used by people of all ages.

norwegian: Mange av de gamle bygningene i sentrum ble revet på 1970-tallet.
english: Many of the old buildings in the city centre were demolished in the 1970s.

norwegian: Oslo er ___ enn Bergen.
english: Oslo is bigger than Bergen.

norwegian: Søsteren min er ___ enn meg.
english: My sister is younger than me.

norwegian: Det er ___ å ta toget enn å kjøre.
english: It is better to take the train than to drive.

norwegian: Denne veien er ___ enn den andre.
english: This road is longer than the other one.

norwegian: Katten er mindre enn hunden.
english: The cat is smaller than the dog.

norwegian: Det er mer interessant å lese enn å se på TV.
english: It is more interesting to read than to watch TV.

norwegian: Været i dag er verre enn i går.
english: The weather today is worse than yesterday.

norwegian: Det er flere studenter på dette kurset enn på det forrige.
english: There are more students on this course than on the previous one.

norwegian: Det er det ___ hotellet i byen.
english: It is the best hotel in the city.

norwegian: Han er den ___ gutten i klassen.
english: He is the tallest boy in the class.

norwegian: Dette er den ___ veien til sentrum.
english: This is the shortest way to the city centre.

norwegian: Det er det ___ været vi har hatt på lenge.
english: It is the worst weather we have had in a long time.

norwegian: Everest er det høyeste fjellet i verden.
english: Everest is the highest mountain in the world.

norwegian: Hun er den beste eleven i skolen.
english: She is the best pupil in the school.

norwegian: Dette er den lengste dagen i året.
english: This is the longest day of the year.

norwegian: Flest studenter foretrekker å studere om morgenen.
english: Most students prefer to study in the morning.

norwegian: Den minste leiligheten i bygget er likevel den dyreste.
english: The smallest apartment in the building is nonetheless the most expensive.

norwegian: Jeg vasker ___ før jeg går på jobb.
english: I wash myself before going to work.

norwegian: De giftet ___ i sommer.
english: They got married this summer.

norwegian: Du må kle på ___ raskt, bussen kommer snart.
english: You must get dressed quickly, the bus is coming soon.

norwegian: Vi må skynde ___ hvis vi skal rekke toget.
english: We must hurry if we are to catch the train.

norwegian: Han setter seg ned og slapper av.
english: He sits down and relaxes.

norwegian: Barnet vasker seg selv nå.
english: The child washes themselves now.

norwegian: De kledde på seg og gikk ut i kulden.
english: They got dressed and went out into the cold.

norwegian: Vi satte oss ved vinduet og bestilte kaffe.
english: We sat down by the window and ordered coffee.

norwegian: Hun giftet seg med en norsk mann og flyttet til Tromsø.
english: She got married to a Norwegian man and moved to Tromsø.

norwegian: Kan du hjelpe ___?
english: Can you help me?

norwegian: Vi snakket med ___ i går.
english: We spoke with her yesterday.

norwegian: Læreren forklarte ___ oppgaven to ganger.
english: The teacher explained the task to them twice.

norwegian: Er det mulig å sende brevet til ___?
english: Is it possible to send the letter to us?

norwegian: Jeg ser ham i parken hver dag.
english: I see him in the park every day.

norwegian: Kan du gi meg den boken?
english: Can you give me that book?

norwegian: Vi inviterte deg til festen, men du kom ikke.
english: We invited you to the party, but you didn't come.

norwegian: Jeg ringte henne, men hun svarte ikke.
english: I called her, but she didn't answer.

norwegian: Sjefen ba oss om å fullføre rapporten innen fredag.
english: The boss asked us to finish the report by Friday.

norwegian: Jeg vet at hun ___ liker fisk.
english: I know that she does not like fish.

norwegian: Han gikk hjem ___ han var trøtt.
english: He went home because he was tired.

norwegian: Jeg tror at hun ___ kommer i morgen.
english: I think that she will not come tomorrow.

norwegian: Vi spiste middag ___ filmen var ferdig.
english: We ate dinner after the film was finished.

norwegian: Han sa at han ikke kunne hjelpe i dag.
english: He said that he couldn't help today.

norwegian: Jeg vet ikke om hun er hjemme nå.
english: I don't know if she is home now.

norwegian: Hun ble hjemme fordi hun ikke følte seg bra.
english: She stayed home because she didn't feel well.

norwegian: Selv om det regnet, gikk vi en tur.
english: Even though it was raining, we went for a walk.

norwegian: Når han ikke er på jobb, liker han å lese bøker.
english: When he is not at work, he likes to read books.

norwegian: Bilen ___ han kjøper er veldig dyr.
english: The car that he is buying is very expensive.

norwegian: Mannen ___ bor her er legen min.
english: The man who lives here is my doctor.

norwegian: Jenta ___ bor ved siden av oss heter Maja.
english: The girl who lives next to us is called Maja.

norwegian: Boken ___ jeg leste i helgen var veldig spennende.
english: The book that I read over the weekend was very exciting.

norwegian: Vennen som jeg møtte på skolen heter Lars.
english: The friend that I met at school is called Lars.

norwegian: Filmen som vi så i går var morsom.
english: The film that we watched yesterday was funny.

norwegian: Det er en butikk som selger norske produkter i sentrum.
english: There is a shop that sells Norwegian products in the city centre.

norwegian: Kollegaen som han snakker om bor i Bergen.
english: The colleague that he is talking about lives in Bergen.

norwegian: Det er det stedet som hun alltid vil besøke igjen.
english: That is the place that she always wants to visit again.

norwegian: Jeg liker kaffe, ___ ikke te.
english: I like coffee but not tea.

norwegian: Hun er sliten, ___ hun jobber videre.
english: She is tired, but she keeps working.

norwegian: Vi kan gå til fots ___ ta bussen.
english: We can walk or take the bus.

norwegian: ___ han er trøtt, jobber han alltid hardt.
english: Even though he is tired, he always works hard.

norwegian: Han ville hjelpe, men han hadde ikke tid.
english: He wanted to help, but he didn't have time.

norwegian: Jeg var sulten, så jeg spiste en sandwich.
english: I was hungry, so I ate a sandwich.

norwegian: Mens hun studerte, lyttet han til musikk.
english: While she was studying, he was listening to music.

norwegian: Siden jeg ikke har bil, tar jeg alltid toget.
english: Since I don't have a car, I always take the train.

norwegian: Vi ble sittende ute selv om det ble kaldt etter solnedgang.
english: We stayed outside even though it got cold after sunset.

norwegian: Jeg var ___ bestemoren min i helgen.
english: I was at my grandmother's place over the weekend.

norwegian: Rapporten må leveres ___ mandag.
english: The report must be submitted by Monday.

norwegian: Parken ligger rett ___ biblioteket.
english: The park is right across from the library.

norwegian: Det var stor uenighet ___ deltakerne på møtet.
english: There was great disagreement among the participants at the meeting.

norwegian: Vi spiste middag hos vennen min i går kveld.
english: We had dinner at my friend's place yesterday evening.

norwegian: De gikk langs elven og snakket i timevis.
english: They walked along the river and talked for hours.

norwegian: Skolen ligger mellom kirken og rådhuset.
english: The school is located between the church and the town hall.

norwegian: Det var mange turister rundt fontenen i sentrum.
english: There were many tourists around the fountain in the city centre.

norwegian: Du må registrere deg innen to uker etter ankomst.
english: You must register within two weeks of arrival.

norwegian: Vi møtes ___ mandag klokken ti.
english: We are meeting on Monday at ten o'clock.

norwegian: Jeg bodde i London ___ tre år.
english: I lived in London for three years.

norwegian: Familien besøker oss ___ sommeren.
english: The family visits us in the summer.

norwegian: Hun begynte på universitetet ___ to år siden.
english: She started university two years ago.

norwegian: Butikken åpner klokken ni på lørdag.
english: The shop opens at nine o'clock on Saturday.

norwegian: Jeg trener av og til, men ikke hver dag.
english: I exercise occasionally, but not every day.

norwegian: De reiser til Spania om to uker.
english: They are travelling to Spain in two weeks.

norwegian: Han ringte meg for tre dager siden og ba om hjelp.
english: He called me three days ago and asked for help.

norwegian: Møtet varer i to timer, fra halv tre til halv fem.
english: The meeting lasts two hours, from half past two to half past four.

norwegian: Du ___ ikke betale for det — det er gratis.
english: You don't need to pay for it — it's free.

norwegian: Du ___ spise mer grønnsaker — det er sunt.
english: You should eat more vegetables — it's healthy.

norwegian: Jeg ___ ikke hoppe fra det høye brettet.
english: I don't dare to jump from the high diving board.

norwegian: Jeg ___ ha vært mer forsiktig.
english: I should have been more careful.

norwegian: Du burde ringe legen hvis du fortsatt har feber.
english: You should call the doctor if you still have a fever.

norwegian: Du trenger ikke å hjelpe meg — jeg klarer det selv.
english: You don't need to help me — I can manage on my own.

norwegian: Jeg orker ikke lage middag i dag — vi bestiller pizza.
english: I don't have the energy to cook dinner today — we're ordering pizza.

norwegian: Hun torde endelig si hva hun mente på møtet.
english: She finally dared to say what she thought at the meeting.

norwegian: Vi burde ha tatt bussen — bilen var for full.
english: We should have taken the bus — the car was too full.

norwegian: Hun ___ ned på benken og ventet på bussen.
english: She sat down on the bench and waited for the bus.

norwegian: Han ___ en interessant bok om norsk historie.
english: He found an interesting book about Norwegian history.

norwegian: Barnet ___ og slo seg på kneet.
english: The child fell and hurt its knee.

norwegian: Hun ___ et brev til venninnen sin i utlandet.
english: She wrote a letter to her friend abroad.

norwegian: Vi drakk kaffe og spiste kake i stuen hele ettermiddagen.
english: We drank coffee and ate cake in the living room all afternoon.

norwegian: Han lå i sengen og hørte på musikk hele morgenen.
english: He lay in bed and listened to music all morning.

norwegian: De løp til stasjonen, men de holdt igjen toget i likevel.
english: They ran to the station, but they still missed the train.

norwegian: Hun bar handleposene opp trappene uten å klage.
english: She carried the shopping bags up the stairs without complaining.

norwegian: Han sto ved vinduet og så utover fjorden.
english: He stood by the window and looked out over the fjord.

norwegian: Hun slo rekorden på hundre meter med ett sekund.
english: She broke the hundred-metre record by one second.

english: She said that she would be at work the next day.

english: He said that he had been very tired after work.

norwegian: Hun spurte om jeg ___ komme på festen.
english: She asked whether I wanted to come to the party.

norwegian: Han fortalte at han ___ opp i Bergen.
english: He said that he had grown up in Bergen.

norwegian: Hun sa at hun ikke likte regn.
english: She said that she didn't like rain.

norwegian: Han spurte om vi hadde spist middag.
english: He asked whether we had eaten dinner.

norwegian: Læreren sa at elevene måtte lese kapittel tre til neste gang.
english: The teacher said that the students had to read chapter three for next time.

norwegian: Han fortalte at han kjente henne fra universitetet.
english: He said that he knew her from university.

norwegian: Hun sa at hun hadde glemt å kjøpe melk.
english: She said that she had forgotten to buy milk.

norwegian: Hvis du ___ tidlig, rekker du toget.
english: If you leave early, you will catch the train.

norwegian: Hvis jeg ___ deg, ville jeg studere mer.
english: If I were you, I would study more.

norwegian: Om det ___ fint vær i morgen, går vi en tur.
english: If the weather is nice tomorrow, we will go for a walk.

norwegian: Hvis jeg ___ mer tid, ville jeg lære å spille gitar.
english: If I had more time, I would learn to play guitar.

norwegian: Hvis du er sulten, er det mat i kjøleskapet.
english: If you are hungry, there is food in the fridge.

english: If I had money, I would travel to Japan.

english: If it rains tomorrow, we will take the car instead.

english: If she had known that, she would never have said yes.

norwegian: Om du trener regelmessig, vil du føle deg bedre.
english: If you exercise regularly, you will feel better.

english: He will probably come on Thursday.

norwegian: Jeg tror at han ___ vet svaret.
english: I think that he doesn't know the answer.

norwegian: Hun er ___ glad for å høre det.
english: She is certainly happy to hear that.

english: I don't know whether he always comes home today.

norwegian: Det vet du jo allerede.
english: You already know that, of course.

norwegian: Jeg liker ikke at han aldri hører etter.
english: I don't like that he never listens.

norwegian: Hun er nok sliten etter den lange dagen.
english: She is probably tired after the long day.

norwegian: Jeg tror kanskje vi bør vente litt til.
english: I think maybe we should wait a little longer.

norwegian: Han sa at han vel ikke hadde tid til å møtes.

english: This is Lars's house.

english: I use my neighbour's car for work.

english: We found the teacher's bag on the bench.

norwegian: Marias søster bor i Bergen.
english: Maria's sister lives in Bergen.

norwegian: Boken til Kari er veldig interessant.
english: Kari's book is very interesting.

norwegian: Oslos gater er veldig brede.
english: Oslo's streets are very wide.

english: I borrowed my brother's bicycle.

norwegian: Kongens palasset ligger i sentrum.
english: The king's palace is located in the centre.

english: It is fun to play football with friends.

norwegian: Jeg begynte ___ lære norsk i fjor.
english: I started to learn Norwegian last year.

norwegian: Jeg hørte henne ___ i dusjen.
english: I heard her sing in the shower.

english: It is difficult to understand dialects.

english: I saw the children play in the park.

english: They promised to come early.

english: We met at the train station.

english: It is dangerous to cycle without a helmet.

norwegian: Kjøkkenbenken er laget av tre.
english: The kitchen counter is made of wood.

norwegian: Det er naturlig å føle seg nervøs.
english: It is natural to feel nervous.

norwegian: Forståelsen av grammatikk er nødvendig.
english: The understanding of grammar is necessary.

norwegian: Bokhyllen er full av bøker.
english: The bookshelf is full of books.

english: It is the first time I visit Tromsø.

norwegian: Hun bor i ___ etasje.
english: She lives on the third floor.

norwegian: ___ av melken er igjen i flasken.
english: Half of the milk is left in the bottle.

norwegian: ___ av elevene hadde gjort leksene.
english: Three quarters of the pupils had done the homework.

norwegian: Det andre laget vant kampen.
english: The second team won the match.

norwegian: Han ble født i to tusen og tre.
english: He was born in 2003.

norwegian: Dette er den tiende dagen av ferien.
english: This is the tenth day of the holiday.

norwegian: En tredjedel av befolkningen bor i byene.
english: One third of the population lives in the cities.

norwegian: Tusenvis av turister besøker Norge hvert år.
english: Thousands of tourists visit Norway every year.

norwegian: ___ døren, vær så snill!
english: Close the door, please!

norwegian: ___ ikke på gresset!
english: Do not walk on the grass!

norwegian: ___ hånden din før du spiser!
english: Wash your hands before you eat!

english: Do not drive so fast!

english: Eat up your food!

english: Please sit down.

english: Read the instructions carefully!

english: Write down your address here.

norwegian: Ikke glem passet ditt hjemme!
english: Do not forget your passport at home!
